fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and operational efficiency on this property may benefit from strategic adjustments. Opening up clear and unobstructed pathways by reducing dense vegetation can aid responders in locating and reaching the main building swiftly. Addressing the limited rear access by evaluating options for establishing an additional or alternative access route at the rear of the property could facilitate easier movement for emergency personnel. Ensuring that the driveway is free from obstructions and clearly marked might support efficient staging and navigation. These modifications could collectively enhance visibility, accessibility, and the overall safety and effectiveness of emergency operations.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Scattered Trees; Scattered Shrubs; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Defensible Space; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Side Access
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing vegetation management around the structures by decreasing the amount of nearby vegetation could improve defensible space and reduce potential structure exposure. Increasing the separation between tree clusters and scattered shrubs and the residence may help in reducing fuel continuity. Maintenance of open terrain around the property can be achieved by preserving these areas to act as natural fuel breaks. Additionally, focusing efforts on managing vegetation along the vegetated perimeter and improving side access paths may bolster firefighter access and suppression activities. Regular maintenance of these open spaces, as well as the strategic reduction of nearby vegetation concentrations, would support long-term wildfire resilience and overall property preparedness.
